title: Service Registry and Dependency Injection
description: Framework registers, configures, and injects connector services at runtime via an IoC container pattern
actors: ConnectorFramework (initiates registry, registers BeanRegistry at startup)
ServiceProvider (pluggable factory registered per service type)
Service (any component implementing Service marker interface)
primary_use_cases: Register a pre-built service instance (registerService with existing object)
Register a service provider for lazy initialization (registerServiceProvider)
Retrieve / lazily initialize a service by class type (getService)
Safely retrieve a service without exception (tryGetService → null on miss)
Stop / close all services in reverse registration order (close)
Throw UnknownServiceException when service not found
Throw ServiceDependencyException when required injection fails
Provide SnapshotterService with resolved Snapshotter, SnapshotQuery, SnapshotLock

key_classes.name: ServiceRegistry
methods: default <T> T tryGetService(Class<T> serviceClass) — swallows UnknownServiceException

key_classes.name: DefaultServiceRegistry
key_methods: getService(Class<T>) — double-checked locking, lazy init via initializeService
close() — clears caches, iterates registrations in reverse, calls stopService
doInjections — reflects on @InjectService methods, calls doInjection per method
doInjection — resolves service type, calls getService recursively, invokes setter via reflection

key_classes.name: ServiceRegistration<T>
fields: ServiceProvider<T> serviceProvider (nullable — null for pre-built instances)
constructors: ServiceRegistration(Class<T>, T service) — wraps existing instance

key_classes.name: SnapshotterServiceProvider
createService_flow: 4. ServiceLoader.load(Snapshotter.class) → filter by name match → exactly one required

key_classes.name: UnknownServiceException
key_classes.trigger: thrown by getService() when no registration found for serviceClass

key_classes.name: ServiceDependencyException
key_classes.trigger: thrown by doInjection() when @InjectService(required=true) service missing, or method invocation fails

spi_interfaces: Closeable — close(): called during registry shutdown
InjectService — method annotation: service() class, required() flag (default true)
BeanRegistryAware — injectBeanRegistry(BeanRegistry): used by custom Snapshotter impls
key_relationships: ServiceRegistration holds ServiceProvider<T> OR pre-built Service instance
SnapshotterServiceProvider depends_on BeanRegistry, SnapshotQuery, SnapshotLock (via registry)
Service initialization order enforced by recursive getService calls during doInjection
service_lifecycle_phases: 1. REGISTER — registerServiceProvider() stores ServiceRegistration (no instantiation yet)
2. LAZY INIT (on first getService) — createService via provider → registerService
3. INJECT — reflect @InjectService methods, recursively resolve + inject dependencies
exception_scenarios: createService() with null ServiceProvider (pre-built instance registration) → UnknownServiceException
doInjection() with required=true and missing service → ServiceDependencyException
doInjection() with unexpected param count → ServiceDependencyException
important_notes: @ThreadSafe: DefaultServiceRegistry uses ConcurrentHashMap + synchronized blocks for lazy init (double-checked locking pattern)
BeanRegistry is the only service pre-registered at construction time
ServiceProvider pattern decouples registration from instantiation — supports lazy init
tryGetService is the null-safe variant; callers use it when a service is optional
SnapshotterServiceProvider uses Java ServiceLoader for Snapshotter discovery (SPI)
Snapshot mode 'custom' + customName allows user-provided Snapshotter class names
All SPI interfaces (Configurable, Startable, Closeable, ServiceRegistryAware) are opt-in — checked via instanceof
